name: get_granules
description:
Search NASA CMR granules for a specific parent collection and return one page of lightweight normalized results plus pagination metadata.

IMPORTANT — data availability checks: Without temporal and/or spatial filters, results represent ALL granules ever archived in the collection, which may span decades and the entire globe. total_hits without filters tells you the full archive size, NOT whether data exists for a specific area or time period. To answer 'is data available for region X during period Y?', you MUST provide the relevant filters — otherwise the answer will always appear affirmative even when no data covers that area or time.

Key parameters:
- collection_concept_id: required parent collection concept ID
- temporal_start_date / temporal_end_date: filter to granules overlapping this time window — always set when the user specifies a time period
- spatial_wkt_geometry: filter to granules intersecting this area — always set when the user specifies a geographic region

Pagination:
- page_size controls results per page (max 2000)
- search_after requests the next page from a previous response

Tips:
- For any area- or time-specific availability question, provide both temporal and spatial filters
- total_hits in the response reflects the filtered count — zero means no data for that combination
- Use search_after for deep paging through long result sets

spatiotemporal.description: Filter granules within time and space
